blocks.text: Psalm 30
Ascribe to the Lord, O heavenly beings, ascribe to the Lord glory and strength.

Ps 29: Acknowledging the Lord’s might in the storm. A hymn in which heavenly beings praise the of the storm who brings life‐giving rain; many scholars think that this was a northern Canaanite psalm (see the places named in vv. 5–8), adapted by early Israel.
1 : The (lit., “sons of gods”) are summoned to acknowledge the returning victorious to the assembly (cf. 89.5–7).
Ascribe to the Lord the glory of his name; worship the Lord in holy splendor.
2 : , perhaps “when the Holy One appears.”
The voice of the Lord is over the waters; the God of glory thunders, the Lord, over mighty waters.

3 : Symbolizing a cosmic event, a massive storm roars in from the Mediterranean and hits the coast with deafening thunderclaps ( , used seven times in this section), drenching rain, and lightning flashes. The luminous thundercloud is imagined as the ’s chariot, and the thunder, rain, and lightning are his soldiers. They sweep across the trees and appear to shatter and shake the forested mountain; earthquake also accompanies a divine appearance or theophany (e.g., Pss 18.7; 114.7).
The voice of the Lord is powerful; the voice of the Lord is full of majesty.
The voice of the Lord breaks the cedars; the Lord breaks the cedars of Lebanon.
He makes Lebanon skip like a calf, and Sirion like a young wild ox.
6 : , Mount Hermon (see Deut 3.9), at the southern border of .
The voice of the Lord flashes forth flames of fire.
The voice of the Lord shakes the wilderness; the Lord shakes the wilderness of Kadesh.
8 : , the desert east of the city of Kadesh in central western Syria.
The voice of the Lord causes the oaks to whirl, and strips the forest bare; and in his temple all say, “Glory!”
The Lord sits enthroned over the flood; the Lord sits enthroned as king forever.
10 : , ancient myths sometimes depicted the storm god defeating hostile Sea and building his palace upon its body.
May the Lord give strength to his people! May the Lord bless his people with peace!
11 : The heavenly beings pray that the divine king would extend the fruits of victory to Israel, the ’s special people.
